title: ranunculus acris var. villosus
intro: Ranunculus acris var. villosus, also known as the hairy buttercup, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Ranunculaceae family. The hairy buttercup has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be utilized to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots, leaves, and flowers are often used to create infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be applied topically or consumed internally to leverage its medicinal benefits. The hairy buttercup is believed to offer health benefits such as improved digestion, reduced inflammation, and enhanced immune function, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
